name: WIFIREWONV1
summary: Repository indexing entry for the ``WIFIREWONV1`` project. Index entry in the KFIOSA catalog. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner. Attribution and provenance only; audit code, releases, and licence before use in the lab or production environment.

usage_sections.content: Run on Bruce firmware device (ESP32/Flipper-style): load WIFIRECONv1.js via the Bruce script runner; no CLI args. Not invokable on a host OS.

examples.command: Run on Bruce firmware device (ESP32/Flipper-style): load WIFIRECONv1.js via the Bruce script runner; no CLI args. Not invokable on a host OS.

dependencies: Bruce firmware JS runtime (wifi, display, keyboard built-in modules)
algorithms: Passive WiFi scan via platform wifi.scan() API (no monitor mode, no channel hop)
OUI vendor lookup via hardcoded 8-char MAC-prefix table (Cisco, Apple, Google, TP-Link, Netgear, etc.)
Encryption risk classification: OPEN/WEP=critical, WPA/WPA2-mixed/WAPI=warn, else=safe
Static attack-surface/mitigation reference text keyed to encryption type (e.g. PMKID attack, 4-way handshake)
produces: On-device display only: list view of networks grouped FLAGGED/SECURE with SSID, BSSID, encryption type, OUI vendor, risk badges [!!]/[ !]/[ v]
Detail view: protocol description, static attack-surface summary, mitigation recommendations
No file/serial/network output; RSSI and stations explicitly unavailable via API
novelty: none — thin wrapper. It does not shell out to airodump-ng/wash/hcxtools/scapy; it calls the Bruce firmware's wifi.scan() and renders results with a hardcoded OUI table and static risk labels. The OUI-enrich + encryption-risk-badge logic is trivial and already covered by airodump-ng output + KFIOSA's OUI enrichment. The only distinctive aspect is the embedded display UI, which is not portable to Kali.

trust.warning: External community repo. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use. Catalogued for the AI's awareness; novel algorithms are implemented in core/modules/catalog_recon.py, not shelled out to this repo's binaries.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
